title: 1.2-Gigawatt Data Center Near Evanston In Talks With Meta And Microsoft
summary: Uinta County Commissioners approved two letters of support for Prometheus Hyperscale addressed to Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Microsoft VP Noelle Walsh. University of Wyoming economic analysis shows a $3.1 billion economic impact through 2032 and 600 direct employees.
key_claims: Prometheus Hyperscale is in deep discussions with Meta and Microsoft as potential tenants for the 1.2 GW Evanston data center, with a projected $3.1 billion economic impact.

title: Evanston Company Creates Plan To Train, Hire, Staff Local Workforce For Data Centers
summary: Prometheus Hyperscale has put together plans to train, hire, and retain a skilled local workforce through its Build Wyoming initiative, including a $10,000 donation to kickstart a vocational workforce pipeline at Eastern Wyoming College.
key_claims: Build Wyoming initiative aims to create a vocational pipeline for data center workers, prioritizing Wyoming residents for all jobs at Prometheus campuses.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale Sets New National Standard For Data Center Sites
summary: Prometheus Hyperscale announced it will enter into formal Community Benefit Agreements for each of its data center developments in Wyoming, becoming the first U.S. data center developer to commit to such agreements at scale.
key_claims: Prometheus becomes the first U.S. data center developer to commit to community benefit agreements at scale, with local workforce priorities embedded.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale Launches 'Build Wyoming' Initiative to Create Local Job Opportunities
summary: Prometheus Hyperscale launched the Build Wyoming initiative focused on electricians, welders, pipefitters, operators, and technicians to build AI infrastructure, prioritizing Wyoming residents.
key_claims: Build Wyoming initiative prioritizes local hiring for skilled trades positions needed to build and operate AI infrastructure.

title: County Officials Sign Letters of Support for Incoming Data Center
summary: Uinta County Economic Development Director Gary Welling requested commissioners approve and sign letters of support for Prometheus Hyperscale to help attract Meta and Microsoft as tenants.
key_claims: Uinta County officials formally endorsed Prometheus Hyperscale's recruitment of Meta and Microsoft as anchor tenants.

title: Local Lookout: Uinta County Preps for Data Center Workers
summary: Uinta County is preparing infrastructure and workforce housing to accommodate data center construction workers as the Prometheus Hyperscale project moves forward.
key_claims: Uinta County is preparing infrastructure for workforce housing to handle up to 3,000 construction workers.

title: Casper Lands Massive 1.5-Gigawatt Data Center Project on Nearby Ranch
summary: Prometheus Hyperscale announced a 1.5-gigawatt data center near Casper on ranch land straddling Natrona and Converse counties, with a minimum $500 million initial investment and potential for 600 employees.
key_claims: Prometheus Hyperscale's second Wyoming campus will deliver 1.5 GW of IT capacity with $500M initial investment and up to $3.1 billion economic impact.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale Plans Data Hall, Energy Infrastructure in Natrona County
summary: Local coverage of Prometheus Hyperscale's partnership with Spiritus and Casper Carbon Capture to build carbon-negative data halls and energy infrastructure in Natrona County.
key_claims: Partnership with Spiritus and Casper Carbon Capture targets first IT-ready power for the Casper campus in 2026.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale, Spiritus, and Casper Carbon Capture Announce Strategic Partnership to Build Carbon-Negative Data Halls
summary: Three-company partnership for a carbon-negative digital infrastructure campus with at least $500 million initial investment, delivering up to 1.5 GW of IT-ready capacity.
key_claims: The partnership aims to build the world's first large-scale carbon-negative data center campus with integrated direct air capture and CO2 sequestration.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale Works with Carbon Capture Firms on New Data Center Campus in Wyoming
summary: Industry trade publication coverage of the Prometheus/Spiritus/Casper Carbon Capture partnership for the $500M+ carbon-negative data center campus near Casper.
key_claims: Industry analysts note the Casper campus represents a novel approach combining AI compute with carbon removal at scale.

title: Wyoming To Host A Major New Carbon-Negative Digital Infrastructure Campus
summary: Environmental/carbon-focused publication covering the Casper area carbon-negative data center campus with integrated direct air capture and CO2 sequestration.
key_claims: The Casper campus integrates carbon removal into data center operations, aiming for carbon-negative status rather than merely carbon-neutral.

title: Year in Review Part II: Oil City's Top 10 Stories for 2025
summary: Oil City News year-in-review including the Prometheus Hyperscale Casper data center announcement among the top stories of 2025 for the Casper area.
key_claims: The Prometheus Hyperscale Casper campus announcement was among the top stories for the Casper region in 2025.

title: Crusoe, Tallgrass Get Green Light for Wyoming AI Energy Campus
summary: Laramie County commissioners unanimously approved Crusoe Energy's Project Jade AI data center campus and Tallgrass Energy's associated power generation hub, despite neighbor concerns about water and views.
key_claims: Laramie County commissioners unanimously approved Project Jade despite neighbor concerns about water contamination and lost views.

title: Huge Cheyenne Data Center Grows To 2.7 Gigawatts -- Nearly Triple State Power Use
summary: Project Jade expanded to 2.7 GW, nearly triple Wyoming's total current power use. Total project cost could reach $50 billion, with potential to scale to 10 GW.
key_claims: Project Jade has grown to 2.7 GW -- nearly triple Wyoming's entire current power use -- and could eventually scale to 10 GW.

title: Wyoming County Approves Construction of What Could Become the Largest Data Center in US
summary: Environmental reporting on Laramie County's approval of Project Jade, noting concerns about water regulation gaps and deep well drilling near the Ogallala Aquifer.
key_claims: Concerns raised about lack of water regulation and potential impacts to the Ogallala Aquifer from deep well drilling for data center cooling.

title: Wyoming County Approves Construction of What Could Become the Largest Data Center in the US
summary: Wyoming Public Media's coverage of the Project Jade approval, including community concerns about water supply and lack of comprehensive data center regulations.
key_claims: The PSC expressed frustration at lacking regulatory authority over data center projects of this unprecedented scale.

title: Laramie County Approves Construction of What Could Become the Largest Data Center in US
summary: WyoFile's detailed coverage of the approval process, community objections from Hyndman Homesites HOA, and regulatory gaps for Project Jade.
key_claims: Community members objected to potential aquifer contamination, emissions, and noise from the massive data center and power plant.

title: Wyoming Approves Data Center Campus That Includes 2.7 GW of New Natural Gas-Fired Generation
summary: Technical analysis of the approved 2.7 GW gas-fired power plant that will serve the Crusoe/Tallgrass data center campus, a $7 billion energy infrastructure investment.
key_claims: Tallgrass will invest $7 billion in natural gas-fired generation capacity to power the data center campus independently of the state grid.

title: Crusoe Gets Go-Ahead for 1.8GW Data Center Campus and Power Plant in Cheyenne, Wyoming
summary: DCD trade coverage of the Crusoe/Tallgrass approval for the 1.8GW data center and power plant on 600 acres near Terry Ranch Road in Laramie County.
key_claims: The campus will include five data center buildings of roughly 805,300 sqft each, with construction beginning in Q1 2026.

title: Wyoming Just Greenlit America's Largest Data Center Project, and Locals Are Bracing for Impact
summary: National tech coverage focusing on local opposition, including concerns from the Hyndman Homesites HOA about aquifer wells, emissions, wastewater, noise, and light pollution.
key_claims: Local residents are bracing for impacts including aquifer contamination, emissions, noise, and light pollution, with only ~400 permanent jobs despite 5,000 construction jobs.

title: Laramie County Commission Unanimously Approves Massive AI Data Center
summary: Wyoming Public Media coverage of the commission vote, reporting that the facility is designed to eventually scale to 10 GW, the largest single AI campus in the U.S.
key_claims: Laramie County commissioners voted unanimously to approve Project Jade, designed to eventually scale to 10 GW.

title: County Commissioners Approve Data Center, Power Plant Facilities South of Cheyenne
summary: Local coverage of the county commissioners' approval of both the data center and power plant, including details on resident concerns about aquifer impacts and emissions.
key_claims: Residents raised concerns about deep well drilling, gas turbine emissions, wastewater ponds, and traffic impacts during the public hearing.

title: $1.2 Billion Data Center Breaks Ground In Cheyenne, CEO Stresses Low Water Impact
summary: Related Digital broke ground on a $1.2 billion data center with CoreWeave as anchor tenant, emphasizing air-cooled chillers with minimal water use and a $3.5M contribution for 184 affordable housing units.
key_claims: Related Digital CEO said bathrooms would be the largest water consumer on site; company also contributed $3.5M for affordable housing.

title: $1.2 Billion Data Center Breaks Ground in Cheyenne, Promises Water-Free Operations
summary: Related Digital's $1.2 billion data center broke ground with CoreWeave as first customer, promising water-free cooling operations using high-efficiency air-cooled chillers.
key_claims: The 302 MW facility promises essentially water-free operations through air-cooled chiller technology.

title: How One Data Center Being Built in Cheyenne Sees Their Role
summary: Interview with Related Digital's SVP about their closed-loop, air-cooled cooling system, and how residents wonder about impacts on water, energy, and air pollution.
key_claims: Related Digital emphasizes its closed-loop cooling and community engagement approach, though residents still have concerns about cumulative impacts.

title: Data Centers, Business Parks, And Hotels Fuel Cheyenne Building Boom
summary: Cheyenne is being reshaped by a building boom driven by data centers, business parks, and hotels. Laramie County extended a regulatory pause through August 2026 for data center land-use regulations.
key_claims: Cheyenne is experiencing a construction boom with Laramie County extending a regulatory pause through August 2026 to develop data center land-use rules.

title: Cheyenne's Data-Center Boom
summary: Overview: 12 operational data centers, 5 under construction, 43 announced projects in the Cheyenne area. Project Jade's 4M sqft campus could require up to 10 GW.
key_claims: Cheyenne has 12 operational data centers, 5 under construction, and 43 announced projects, making it a national hub for AI infrastructure.

title: (OPINION) Cheyenne Has an Opportunity in Responsible Data Center Development
summary: Opinion column arguing Cheyenne can benefit from data center development if done responsibly, with attention to community impacts and resource use.
key_claims: Argues Cheyenne should embrace data centers while requiring transparency, community benefits, and resource protections.

title: Poll: What Are The Biggest Issues Facing Cheyenne In 2026?
summary: Community poll finding many residents think Cheyenne has gone overboard on data centers, with fears about utility bill impacts. Over 200 responses cited wages, cost of living, and crime.
key_claims: Community poll reveals significant concern that Cheyenne has overcommitted to data centers, with residents worried about rising utility costs and quality of life.

title: Bigger Data Centers Are Coming. How Can Wyoming Get Ahead of the Boom?
summary: Exploration of Wyoming's regulatory readiness for the data center boom, including the drafted but not introduced 'Extremely Large Electrical Loads' bill and concerns about lack of checks and balances.
key_claims: Wyoming lacks adequate regulatory frameworks for data centers, with a proposed 'Extremely Large Electrical Loads' bill drafted but never introduced.

title: Ratepayer Protection Pledge Aims to Keep Energy Costs Down Amid Data Center Boom
summary: Major tech companies pledged to pay for energy infrastructure needed for data centers, aiming not to pass costs to households. In many cases, the boom has resulted in new natural gas pipelines.
key_claims: Seven major tech companies signed the Ratepayer Protection Pledge committing to pay for energy infrastructure needed by their data centers.

title: Wyoming's Data Center Boom Sparks Fears Of Soaring Electric Rates
summary: Concerns that data center energy demands could drive residential electricity rates higher, despite Wyoming statute 37-3-116 protecting existing ratepayers.
key_claims: Wyoming statute 37-3-116 states data center customers shall not obligate existing ratepayers for utility investment costs, but enforcement remains uncertain.

title: Will Data Centers Mean Higher Power Costs in Wyoming?
summary: AARP Wyoming raises concerns about seniors and fixed-income residents being disproportionately affected by data center-driven electricity rate increases.
key_claims: Seniors and fixed-income Wyoming residents are disproportionately at risk from data center-driven electricity rate increases.

title: The Hidden Toll of AI Data Centers
summary: Investigation into how utilities may offer data centers secret contracts that shift costs to the public, and how 60% of U.S. data center electricity comes from fossil fuels.
key_claims: Utilities may offer data centers secret contracts that shift costs to the public, with health burdens potentially exceeding $20 billion by 2028.

title: Data Center Boom Threatens to Overload Wyoming's Power System
summary: Data centers seeking up to 10,000 MW risk doubling statewide demand, with little framework for managing facilities that consume the same resources as a small city.
key_claims: Rocky Mountain Power's Wyoming load is just under 1 GW (built over 100 years) but is now being asked to add the equivalent of 10 states' worth of capacity in 3-5 years.

title: Utilities Brace for Staggering Power Demands from AI, Data Centers in Wyoming
summary: Wyoming's electrical generation capacity is about 10,200 MW, with homes using only 40%. Data center projects could eclipse the state's total capacity.
key_claims: Wyoming utilities face over 10 GW of service requests for the next 3-5 years while their existing Wyoming load is under 1 GW.

title: An Enormous Data Center In Wyoming Will Consume 5x More Power Than The State's People
summary: The Crusoe data center at full capacity would draw 87.6 TWh annually -- double Wyoming's total current energy production -- and the ultimate tenant remains unnamed.
key_claims: At full capacity the facility would consume 5x more electricity than all Wyoming homes combined, with the ultimate AI tenant remaining a mystery.

title: Wyoming House Will Hear Bill To Investigate How Data Centers Impact Water Supply
summary: House Bill 90 would give $500,000 to the Wyoming State Engineer to study large-scale industrial water use by data centers. The bill ultimately failed on February 23.
key_claims: HB 90 proposed $500,000 for a study on data center water impacts but failed on February 23, 2026, leaving water usage questions unresolved.

title: The West's Thirsty Data Centers
summary: Analysis of western data center water demands in arid climates. The International Energy Agency predicts data center electricity and water consumption will both double by 2030.
key_claims: Data center water consumption could double by 2030 according to the IEA, raising concerns in arid western states like Wyoming.

title: Does Wyoming Have the Water for Thirsty Data Centers?
summary: Examines whether Wyoming's arid climate can support data center water consumption, noting Cheyenne's 14 data centers currently use about 140 acre-feet per year.
key_claims: Cheyenne's existing 14 data centers use about 140 acre-feet (45.6 million gallons) per year, roughly 1% of Cheyenne's total water usage, but new facilities will demand far more.

title: Data Center Policy in Wyoming -- Needs and Recommendations (White Paper)
summary: Policy white paper calling for mandatory water-neutral design, energy and water disclosure requirements, structured community engagement, and expansion of Industrial Siting Council oversight.
key_claims: Calls for mandatory water-neutral design, energy/water disclosure requirements, and expansion of state Industrial Siting Council oversight for data centers.

title: Wyoming Outdoor Council Director: Legislators Should Enact Laws to Regulate Sprawling Data Centers
summary: Op-ed arguing developers can sidestep state-level review by siting within county-designated industrial parks, and local governments lack technical expertise to evaluate impacts.
key_claims: Data center developers can sidestep state-level review by siting in county industrial parks, leaving local governments without expertise to evaluate environmental impacts.

title: Wyoming's Plumber Shortage Collides With Massive Data Center Boom
summary: Wyoming's plumber and pipefitter shortage is being exacerbated by data center construction; trillion-dollar tech companies offer double the wages.
key_claims: Data center construction is worsening Wyoming's existing plumber shortage, with tech companies offering double the wages of residential contractors.

title: Wyoming's Data Centers Need a Workforce Plan
summary: Editorial arguing Wyoming should tie data center tax incentives to workforce development commitments, with calls for accelerated cybersecurity and cloud technology training.
key_claims: Wyoming lacks a statewide pipeline for cloud operations, cybersecurity, network engineering, and AI infrastructure management workers.

title: Wyoming Attracts Considerable Data Center Investment -- But Could Easily Lose It
summary: Analysis warning Wyoming could lose data center competitiveness if it imposes sales tax on machinery and equipment. Wyoming currently offers sales tax exemptions for qualifying investments.
key_claims: Wyoming has 2.87 data centers per $10 billion GSP (3.7x California's rate) but could lose competitiveness if discriminatory taxes are imposed.

title: State Data Center Legislation in 2026 Tackles Energy and Tax Issues
summary: Over 300 data center bills filed across 30+ states in 2026, marking a national shift from incentive-focused policies to regulatory oversight.
key_claims: Over 300 state data center bills have been filed in 2026, marking a national shift from incentives to regulatory oversight.

title: Wyoming Should Encourage Data-Center Development as Economic Tool
summary: Op-ed arguing Wyoming should embrace data centers as an economic diversification tool to offset declining oil and gas revenues.
key_claims: Data centers represent a unique opportunity for Wyoming to diversify its economy beyond oil and gas dependence.

title: Data Centers Are a Rare Opportunity for Wyoming
summary: Opinion piece arguing data centers represent a unique economic opportunity for Wyoming's post-fossil-fuel economic transition.
key_claims: Data centers offer Wyoming a rare chance at economic diversification in a post-fossil-fuel era.

title: Rural Identity and Data Centers Collide in South Dakota
summary: How rural Western communities are grappling with data center proposals that clash with traditional ranching and agricultural identities, with parallels to Wyoming.
key_claims: Rural Western communities face an identity crisis as massive data center projects clash with traditional ranching and agricultural lifestyles.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale, PureWest Energy and Frontier Infrastructure to Enable Zero-Carbon Power Solution
summary: Partnership to deploy natural gas power at the 1.2GW Evanston campus using PureWest's low-carbon gas from the Green River Basin, with Frontier providing 500,000+ tons/year carbon removal credits.
key_claims: Partnership deploys low-carbon natural gas with 500,000+ tons/year of carbon removal credits to power the flagship Evanston data center.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale to Power Wyoming Data Center with Natural Gas, Will Offset Through Carbon Capture
summary: DCD's coverage of the PureWest/Frontier partnership for zero-carbon operations through natural gas paired with carbon capture and sequestration.
key_claims: Prometheus aims for zero-carbon data center operations through natural gas paired with carbon capture, with deep aquifer cooling reducing energy use by 50%.

title: Prometheus Hyperscale Launches 1.2GW Carbon-Neutral Data Center In Wyoming
summary: Coverage of Prometheus Hyperscale's carbon-neutral data center launch, highlighting the 1.2 GW capacity and zero-carbon operations partnerships.
key_claims: Prometheus Hyperscale's 1.2 GW Evanston campus aims to be the first carbon-neutral hyperscale data center at gigawatt scale.

title: Cheyenne Mayor Welcomes 1.8 Gigawatt Data Center as Cities Race to Fuel AI
summary: Cheyenne's mayor expressed support for the Crusoe/Tallgrass 1.8 GW data center project as cities nationwide compete to host AI infrastructure.
key_claims: Cheyenne's mayor publicly welcomed the 1.8 GW data center as part of the national race to build AI infrastructure.

title: Wyoming Energy Policy Stalls on Third-Party Power Generation, Ratepayer Risk
summary: Wyoming lawmakers face a policy divide on allowing third-party power generation, with concerns about higher costs for existing customers if utilities must still provide backup power.
key_claims: Third-party power generation for data centers creates a policy dilemma: utilities may still bear backup costs that get passed to existing ratepayers.

title: The Big Data Center Buildup
summary: Regional western U.S. publication's in-depth look at the data center buildup across the West, featuring Wyoming prominently alongside other states.
key_claims: The western U.S. is experiencing an unprecedented data center buildup that challenges traditional land use and water allocation patterns.

title: Ratepayer Protection Pledge
summary: President Trump's Ratepayer Protection Pledge, signed by Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, Oracle, and xAI, committing to build/bring/buy their own generation resources.
key_claims: Seven major tech companies pledge to build, bring, or buy their own power generation rather than burdening existing ratepayers.

title: Black Hills, NorthWestern Merger Keyed to Western Power Growth as Data Centers Proliferate
summary: Black Hills Corp's merger with NorthWestern is driven partly by growing power demands from data centers across the western U.S., creating a $15B+ combined utility.
key_claims: The Black Hills-NorthWestern merger creates a $15B+ utility positioned to serve growing data center demand across the western U.S.

title: State Regulators to Appeal Utility's Victory in Wyoming Rate Hike Case
summary: Wyoming PSC filed appeal to the 10th Circuit after a federal judge sided with PacifiCorp in the rate hike case.
key_claims: The PSC is appealing a federal court ruling that sided with PacifiCorp on a contested rate hike, with implications for data center cost allocation.

title: Laramie County OK's Zoning to Allow Massive Switchgrass Industrial Park for Data Center, Power Plant
summary: Laramie County commissioners unanimously approved a zone change for the 1,375-acre Switchgrass Industrial Park, six miles south of Cheyenne, to enable data center and energy infrastructure.
key_claims: Laramie County rezoned 1,375 acres for the Switchgrass Industrial Park to accommodate Project Jade's data center and power plant.

title: Bills, Bills, Bills: A Selection of Bills That Survived (or Didn't) This Legislative Session
summary: Recap of Wyoming's 2026 legislative session, including the fate of data center-related bills such as HB 90 (water study, which failed).
key_claims: HB 90 (data center water study) failed during the 2026 session, leaving Wyoming without a formal study of data center water impacts.

title: Wyoming AI Facility Will Consume More Power Than All the State's Homes Combined
summary: Tech industry coverage of the staggering energy demands of Wyoming's planned AI data centers, with one facility consuming more power than every home in the state.
key_claims: A single Wyoming AI facility will consume more electricity than all 238,000 homes in the state combined.

title: Cheyenne to Host Massive AI Data Center Using More Electricity Than All Wyoming Homes Combined
summary: Energy industry publication covering the renewable energy and grid implications of the massive Cheyenne data center project.
key_claims: The Cheyenne data center's power demand raises questions about whether renewable energy expansion can keep pace with AI infrastructure growth.

title: State Data Center Water Usage Legislation Gains Momentum
summary: National policy analysis of data center water usage legislation across states, including Wyoming's HB 90.
key_claims: Data center water usage legislation is gaining momentum across the country, though Wyoming's HB 90 water study failed.

title: What to Expect From the 2026 Wyoming Legislative Session
summary: Law firm analysis noting data center growth, water usage, and power infrastructure debates were expected to dominate the 2026 session.
key_claims: Data center growth, water usage, and power infrastructure debates were expected to be prominent during Wyoming's 2026 legislative session.

title: Fuel Cell Data Center Powers Wyoming's AI Revolution
summary: Coverage of hydrogen fuel cell technology being explored for Wyoming data center power, building on Microsoft's earlier pilot with Caterpillar.
key_claims: Hydrogen fuel cells are being explored as an alternative power source for Wyoming data centers, building on Microsoft's Cheyenne pilot.

title: Counties Wrestle with Data Centers' Power Consumption
summary: National government publication examining how counties, including those in Wyoming, are grappling with the enormous power consumption demands of data centers.
key_claims: Counties across the U.S. are struggling to manage data center power demands that dwarf existing local consumption.

title: Nation's Largest AI Data Center Coming to Wyoming, Raising Questions About Impacts
summary: Broadcast news coverage raising questions about the environmental and community impacts of what would become the nation's largest AI data center project.
key_claims: The nation's largest AI data center raises questions about environmental and community impacts in Wyoming.
